title: COMEDK UGET 2026 Answer Key Release Date and Schedule

detail.title: COMEDK UGET 2026 Answer Key Release Date and Schedule
detail.short_info: COMEDK UGET Provisional Answer Key 2026 has been released at 11 AM on comedk.org. Check how to download the answer key and response sheet, calculate marks, and submit objections before the last date.

content.text: COMEDK UGET Provisional Answer Key 2026 has been released today at 11 AM on the official website comedk.org, and candidates can download it along with their response sheet by logging into the COMEDK candidate portal.

The Consortium of Medical, Engineering and Dental Colleges of Karnataka (COMEDK) has published the provisional answer key for COMEDK UGET 2026 as per the revised schedule. Candidates who appeared for the engineering entrance exam can now check the official key, compare it with their responses, and estimate their probable scores before the result.

rows: Consortium of Medical, Engineering and Dental Colleges of Karnataka (COMEDK)
Objection window last date and time

sections.heading: COMEDK UGET 2026 Answer Key Release Date and Schedule

content.text: The release of the COMEDK UGET provisional answer key 2026 on May 16, 2026, at 11 AM. Along with the provisional key, COMEDK has also notified the objection window, final answer key date, and result date.
Key dates to remember:

items: COMEDK UGET 2026 exam date: May 9, 2026
Provisional answer key release: May 16, 2026, 11 AM onwards
Last date to challenge provisional answer key: May 18, 2026, up to 2 PM
Final answer key release: May 23, 2026, 2 PM
COMEDK UGET 2026 result date: May 29, 2026, 4 PM (rank cards via login)

sections.heading: How to Download COMEDK UGET Provisional Answer Key 2026

content.text: Students can access the COMEDK UGET provisional answer key 2026 only through the official COMEDK portal using their login credentials. The answer key, question paper and response sheet are all provided in the same login area to make verification easier.
Follow these steps to download the COMEDK UGET 2026 answer key:

items: Visit the official website: comedk.org.
Click on the “Login” or “Candidate Login” link for UGET 2026.
Enter your COMEDK application number/user ID and password.
After successful login, click on the link for “Provisional Answer Key / Response Sheet”.
Select your test session (if required) and download the answer key PDF and response sheet.
Save the PDF to your device and take a printout if needed for offline comparison.

sections.heading: COMEDK UGET 2026 Response Sheet and Question Paper

content.text: COMEDK provides the candidate response sheet along with the provisional answer key so that students can accurately match their answers question-wise. Through the same login, candidates can also access the question paper for their specific slot to verify the exact question ID before raising any objections.
By using the response sheet and official key together, candidates can:

items: Identify correct and incorrect attempts
Estimate their probable raw score
Decide whether any question needs to be challenged during the objection window

sections.heading: How to Calculate Scores Using COMEDK UGET Answer Key 2026

content.text: COMEDK UGET follows a simple marking scheme, which makes it easy for candidates to calculate their approximate score using the provisional answer key.
Basic marking scheme (as per the COMEDK information brochure):

items: Each correct answer carries 1 mark
There is no negative marking for wrong answers
Unattempted questions carry 0 marks

content.text: To calculate your probable score:

items: Count the total number of correct responses using the official answer key.
Multiply the number of correct answers by 1.
Since there is no negative marking, you do not deduct marks for incorrect responses.

content.text: For example, if a candidate has 120 correct answers out of 180 questions, their estimated score will be 120 marks.

sections.heading: COMEDK UGET 2026 Answer Key Objection Process

content.text: COMEDK allows candidates to challenge the provisional answer key within a limited time window by submitting objections online. This process ensures that any genuine errors in the answer key can be corrected before the final result is prepared.

sections.heading: COMEDK UGET 2026 Answer Key Challenge Rules

items: Objections can be submitted only through the official candidate login on comedk.org.
A non-refundable fee of Rs 500 per question challenged is charged; the fee may be refunded if the objection is found valid and accepted by experts.
Candidates must provide proper academic justification or reference for each objection.
Objections submitted after the deadline or without payment/supporting proof are not considered.

sections.heading: Steps to Raise Objection Online

items: Visit comedk.org and log in with your application number and password.
Click on the link for “Raise Objection to Provisional Answer Key”.
Select the question ID you want to challenge and choose the option you believe is correct.
Upload supporting documents or references (such as textbook pages or official material) in the specified format.
Pay Rs 500 per question through the online payment gateway.
Submit the objection and note the reference number for future tracking.

content.text: Once the expert panel reviews all objections, COMEDK will finalize the answer key and publish the final version on the notified date.

sections.heading: COMEDK UGET 2026 Final Answer Key and Result

content.text: After evaluating all valid objections, COMEDK will release the final answer key on May 23, 2026, at 2 PM. No further challenges are allowed after the final key is published, and it serves as the basis for result compilation.

The COMEDK UGET 2026 result and rank cards are scheduled to be announced by May 29, 2026, through the official website. Candidates must download their rank card using their login credentials, as it will be required later during the counselling and seat allotment process.

sections.heading: COMEDK UGET 2026 Eligibility and Next Steps After Answer Key

content.text: COMEDK UGET 2026 is open to candidates who meet the eligibility criteria related to age, qualifying exam, and subject combinations as specified in the official information brochure. Typically, candidates must have passed or appeared in Class 12 or equivalent examination with Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics, meeting the minimum aggregate marks required for engineering courses.
After checking the provisional answer key:

items: Candidates should estimate their probable score and compare it with previous year trends to gauge their chances of getting preferred colleges.
They must track official updates on comedk.org regarding the result, counselling schedule, registration, document verification and choice filling.

sections.heading: COMEDK UGET 2026 Answer Key – Important Instructions for Candidates

content.text: Candidates should use the answer key only through the official portal and avoid relying on unofficial keys when raising objections. It is advisable to verify question IDs carefully from the question paper and response sheet before submitting any challenge to avoid mistakes.

Students must ensure a stable internet connection and keep their application number and password handy while downloading the answer key and response sheet. They should also adhere strictly to the objection deadline, as requests received after the cut-off time will not be accepted by COMEDK.
